I can understand your passion.
I grew up on a farm as well, spent my time in the wool shed.
Yeah. Um, uh, it feels like going home, um, with the,
coming back to the issue around, you know,
the particular word that Mr.
McKelvy used around subsidies
and trying to get our products into the New Zealand market
and incentivize them, uh, the use, uh,
what work does the ministry do around, um,
trying to support that incentivization without breaching,
um, our trade agreements and,
and getting us into hot water there?
Are you looking for innovative ways to,
to work in that space as well?
Well, I guess our whole investment program as a way of,
um, supporting those companies, that's not
what I call an incentive.
Um, it is something which is consistent
with our international trade obligations.
Um, uh, and uh, whole investment program is a way
of helping the local industry with, um, projects
and accelerating the speed that they can innovate
and the speed that they can get things into new markets.
Thank you. Really appreciate your time today.
Um, as you can see, the committee has a great deal
of interest in this topic,
even the dairy farmers in the room.
Um, so we really appreciate you taking the time
and staying for a couple of extra
minutes to answer our questions.
Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. Thanks.
